headline: What BlackRock, State Street's rival QQQ ETFs mean for investors
summary: BlackRock (BLK) and State Street (SPY) are both launching exchange-traded funds (ETFs) to rival Invesco QQQ (QQQ), which tracks the NASDAQ-100 (^NDX).BNY global head of ETFs Ben Slavin joins Yahoo Finance to discuss what this news means for investors.

headline: Shares of financial services companies are trading higher after President Trump's social media post indicated that the U.S. will work closely with Iran, and the countries have already agreed to several key points in the proposed conflict-resolution plan. The sector is also benefiting from easing energy prices and lower Treasury yields, which are helping to alleviate inflation concerns and support overall market sentiment.

headline: BlackRock, State Street Challenge Invesco's Nasdaq-100 ETF Dominance
summary: BlackRock and State Street have threatened Invesco's decade-long dominance on the Nasdaq-100 ETF space, filing with the SEC to launch competing funds tracking the same index. QQQ is currently the only ETF tracking the Nasdaq 100 ETF and is one of the most successful ETFs with $375.5 billion in AUM. With expense ratios yet to be disclosed, fees may emerge as the biggest win factor, potentially driving meaningful flows and reshaping competition in one of the ETF market's most lucrative segments.
